data.title: Arcbest expects 400 to 500 bps sequential OR improvement in Q2 as it launches ArcBest View in May
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: ArcBest (ARCB) Q1 2026

Management view

  • "The first quarter brought a challenging operating environment with severe winter weather, higher fuel prices and continued uncertainty" (President, CEO & Director Seth Runser), while he also said, "Customer demand has remained steady, and we continue to see improvement in our pipeline."
  • "Against that backdrop, we will launch ArcBest View in May" (President, CEO & Director Runser), describing it as a platform that "enables customers to quote, book and track shipments across our logistics solutions through a single intuitive interface" and adding that "early feedback has been very encouraging."
  • "Deferred price increases averaged 6% in the first quarter, our strongest result since the third quarter of 2022" (President, CEO & Director Runser), and he tied pricing control to mix tools: "the expansion of our dynamic quote pool has given us greater ability to make real-time pricing decisions, allowing us to be more selective and further optimize yield and profitability."
  • "To date, these efforts have generated $32 million in annualized cost savings" (President, CEO & Director Runser) from continuous improvement work, and he added on city route optimization: "To date, the program has delivered $15 million in annualized savings while also improving network efficiency and service."
  • "On a consolidated basis, first quarter revenue was $1 billion, up 3% year-over-year" (Chief Financial Officer Matt Beasley), and he added, "Non-GAAP operating income was $13 million" and "adjusted earnings per share were $0.32."

Outlook

  • "Historically, ABF's non-GAAP operating ratio improved by approximately 350 basis points from the first quarter to the second quarter" (Chief Financial Officer Beasley), and he guided: "Based on current trends, we expect second quarter performance to improve sequentially by approximately 400 to 500 basis points."
  • "Looking ahead, we expect second quarter non-GAAP operating income in Asset-Light to be in the range of approximately $1 million to $3 million" (Chief Financial Officer Beasley), adding the range "reflects continued yield discipline, active cost management and improved productivity performance."
  • Compared with the prior call’s seasonality framing, management moved from "we currently expect our first quarter operating ratio to increase by approximately 100 to 200 basis points sequentially" (Chief Financial Officer Beasley, Q4 call) to an explicit second-quarter improvement range of "approximately 400 to 500 basis points" (Chief Financial Officer Beasley, Q1 call).

Financial results

  • "First quarter revenue was $655 million" in Asset-Based (Chief Financial Officer Beasley), and he said "ABF's operating ratio was 97.3%" alongside volume/mix drivers: "Daily tonnage increased 7%, reflecting a 2% increase in shipments per day and a 5% increase in weight per shipment."
  • "Operating expenses increased for several reasons" (Chief Financial Officer Beasley), citing "additional labor needed to support shipment growth," "annual contract increases in union wage rates," "higher fuel prices" and "increased depreciation expense associated with our equipment investments."
  • In Asset-Light, "First quarter revenue was $378 million" (Chief Financial Officer Beasley), and he emphasized productivity and cost performance: "Selling, general and administrative expense per shipment declined 15% to the lowest level on record" and "shipments per person per day increasing 26%," which he said contributed to "non-GAAP operating income of $3 million."
  • On capital returns and balance sheet, "In the first quarter of 2026, we returned more than $10 million through a combination of share repurchases and dividends" (Chief Financial Officer Beasley), and he added, "We have ample liquidity and a net debt-to-EBITDA ratio that is well below the S&P 500 average."

Q&A

  • Ravi Shanker, Morgan Stanley: asked about management’s comment that conditions are "becoming more constructive"; President, CEO & Director Runser answered, "Manufacturing/ housing continues to pressure our volumes" but added, "Capacity fundamentals continue to move in a more constructive direction" and "PMI move into expansion territory these past 3 months."
  • Christian Wetherbee, Wells Fargo: questioned truckload-to-LTL spillover and regulatory effects; President, CEO & Director Runser said, "spot rates are currently exceeding contract by 15% to 20%" and added, "we expect as we move through second and third quarter, it's probably going to be in the low to mid-double-digit range" for contract increases; Eddie Sorg added, "we have seen a tighter truckload capacity market" and "some early signs of business push into our integrated logistics solutions and LTL."
  • Scott Group, Wolfe Research: asked what drives the Q2 OR improvement and fuel mechanics; Chief Financial Officer Beasley said, "it's across the board where we're seeing outperformance" and added, "The fuel changes are not the primary driver of the outperformance in the second quarter."
  • Brian Ossenbeck, JPMorgan: raised broker liability/safety headlines; President, CEO & Director Runser said, "Safety has really always been fundamentally how ArcBest operates" and "we use a structured compliance-based process to select and monitor third-party carriers," adding, "at this time, we don't expect these developments to change our outlook or our approach."
  • Ariel Rosa, Citigroup: asked how Runser’s approach differs from his predecessor; President, CEO & Director Runser said, "my goal is really to accelerate that progress we've already made" and highlighted "optimizing our sales resources" and that "when ArcBest View launches in May, it's really going to be differentiated in the marketplace."

Sentiment analysis

  • Analysts: slightly negative to neutral, pressing on visibility into demand recovery, fuel lag, mix/weight drivers, and legal/regulatory risk (e.g., Wolfe’s focus on whether guidance was "driving" OR improvement and JPMorgan’s liability scenario setup).
  • Management: slightly positive in prepared remarks, and more explanatory in Q&A, repeatedly emphasizing discipline and uncertainty (e.g., "the timing of the demand inflection still remains uncertain" (President, CEO & Director Runser) and "The fuel changes are not the primary driver" (Chief Financial Officer Beasley)).
  • Versus the prior quarter, the current call used more “constructive” framing ("conditions are becoming more constructive" (President, CEO & Director Runser)) while the prior quarter repeatedly referenced a "prolonged freight recession" (President, CEO & Director Runser, Q4 call).

Quarter-over-quarter comparison

  • Strategy and product emphasis shifted from broad AI highlights to a near-term commercial platform milestone: Q1 introduced "we will launch ArcBest View in May" (President, CEO & Director Runser) after Q4 positioned AI as "a pivotal year for accelerating AI across our organization" (President, CEO & Director Runser, Q4 call).
  • Cost/efficiency progress was updated: Q1 said, "Continuous improvement training has now been implemented across approximately 75% of the network" and "generated $32 million in annualized cost savings" (President, CEO & Director Runser) versus Q4’s "implemented across approximately 60% of the network" and "delivered $24 million in annual cost savings" (President, CEO & Director Runser, Q4 call).
  • Financial tone tightened around Asset-Light: Q4 guided "an operating loss of up to $1 million" for Q1 (Chief Financial Officer Beasley, Q4 call), while Q1 reported "non-GAAP operating income of $3 million" (Chief Financial Officer Beasley) and guided Q2 Asset-Light profit of "$1 million to $3 million" (Chief Financial Officer Beasley).

Risks and concerns

  • "Severe winter weather" and "higher fuel prices" were cited as key near-term pressures (President, CEO & Director Runser), while the CFO warned that "periods of rapid fuel price movement can create short-term timing differences" between surcharge revenue recognition and incurred costs (Chief Financial Officer Beasley).
  • End-market sensitivity remained in focus: "Manufacturing/ housing continues to pressure our volumes" (President, CEO & Director Runser), and he added that housing-related demand affects U-Pack: "it's resulted in fewer household good moves" (President, CEO & Director Runser).
  • Regulatory/legal uncertainty surfaced in truckload brokerage, with management emphasizing mitigation through controls: "carriers that don't meet those requirements are not eligible to move freight for us" (President, CEO & Director Runser).

Final takeaway

Management characterized Q1 as weather- and fuel-challenged but pointed to steadier demand, an improving pipeline, and early truckload tightening signals, while highlighting the May launch of ArcBest View, continued pricing discipline (including 6% deferred increases), and expanding productivity programs. The company guided to a 400 to 500 basis-point sequential improvement in the Asset-Based operating ratio in Q2 and expected Asset-Light Q2 operating income of $1 million to $3 million, while reiterating that fuel volatility can create timing impacts even with surcharge mechanisms.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: The Bank of N.T. Butterfield & Son Non-GAAP EPS of $1.55 beats by $0.15, revenue of $154.5M beats by $4.56M
data.description:
• The Bank of N.T. Butterfield & Son press release (NTB): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.55 beats by $0.15.
  • Revenue of $154.5M (+4.3% Y/Y) beats by $4.56M.
  • Return on average common equity of 22.1% and core return on average tangible common equity1 of 24.1%
  • Net interest margin of 2.75%, cost of deposits of 1.24%
  • Quarterly cash dividend of $0.50 per share for the quarter ended March 31, 2026
  • Repurchases of 0.8 million shares at a total cost of $42.4 million
  • Closed previously announced acquisition of Rawlinson & Hunter Guernsey on April 15, 2026

data.title: Renasant Non-GAAP EPS of $0.93 beats by $0.09, revenue of $273.8M misses by $1.91M
data.description:
• Renasant press release (RNST): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.93 beats by $0.09.
  • Revenue of $273.8M (+60.5% Y/Y) misses by $1.91M.
  • Return on assets was 1.33% for the first quarter of 2026, up from 0.94% in the first quarter of 2025
  • Return on average equity for the first quarter of 2026 was 9.20%, up from 6.25% in the first quarter of 2025
  • Return on average tangible common equity (non-GAAP)(1) was 16.36% for the first quarter of 2026, up from 10.16% in the first quarter of 2025
  • Our efficiency ratio improved to 55.73% for the first quarter of 2026, down from 65.51% in the first quarter of 2025, and the adjusted efficiency ratio (non-GAAP)(1) improved to 52.82% for the first quarter of 2026, down from 64.43% in the first quarter of 2025

data.title: InvenTrust Properties FFO of $0.49 misses by $0.01
data.description:
• InvenTrust Properties press release (IVT): Q1 FFO of $0.49 misses by $0.01.
  • Total income of $82.58M vs. $73.77M a year ago.
  • Leased Occupancy as of March 31, 2026 of 96.4%
  • Executed 64 leases totaling approximately 329,000 square feet of GLA, of which 249,000 square feet was executed at a blended comparable lease spread of 10.5%
  • Acquired two properties and one single-tenant outparcel adjacent to an existing property, totaling approximately 391,000 square feet, for an aggregate acquisition price of approximately $123.0 million
  • Expanded our Sun Belt presence into Nashville, Tennessee with the acquisition of Nashville West

data.title: Veralto Non-GAAP EPS of $1.07 beats by $0.06, revenue of $1.42B beats by $20M
data.description:
• Veralto press release (VLTO): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.07 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $1.42B (+6.8% Y/Y) beats by $20M.
  • Operating cash flow was $182 million and non-GAAP free cash flow was $170 million
  • Capital allocation of ~$1 billion year-to-date:
    • Completed strategic acquisitions of In-Situ and GlobalVision(1) for ~$620 million
    • Repurchased $300 million shares, or 1.3% of outstanding shares(2)
  • Initiated cost optimization program(1) to streamline business processes and enhance operating efficiency:
    • Expect to incur a charge of $85 to $105 million and yield annual savings of $65 to $75 million by 2028

data.title: American Assets Trust FFO of $0.51 beats by $0.01, revenue of $110.59M beats by $0.81M
data.description:
